C001.text: THE BEAN ADDICTION: You’ve spent £400 on ‘Single-Origin’ coffee this year. Your blood is now 30% caffeine and 0% savings.
options: Brew your own beans at home to save £3 per cup.
Switch to a cheaper supermarket blend for weekdays.
Buy an industrial espresso machine on a high-interest credit card.

C002.text: GHOST SUBSCRIPTION: The gym hasn't seen you since 2022, but they still take £45 monthly. You are a silent donor to a room of sweaty metal.
options: Cancel the membership immediately via the app.
Freeze the account for £5 a month while you 'think about it'.
Buy expensive new lifting straps to 'force' yourself to go.

C003.text: 3 AM AMAZON HAUL: You bought a 'Bluetooth-enabled Toaster' while sleep-deprived. Your bread is now smarter than you.
options: Initiate a return and drop it off at a locker tomorrow.
Keep it as a funny conversation piece for guests.
Buy the matching 'Wi-Fi Kettle' to complete the set.

C004.text: THE AVOCADO INCIDENT: You spent £18 on toast. The internet says this is why you’ll never own a home.
options: Buy a bag of avocados at the market and toast your own bread.
Only order it on special occasions or 'payday'.
Buy 'Avocado Toast' scented candles to feel the luxury at home.

C005.text: DELIVEROO DEPENDENCY: You ordered a single milkshake from 5 miles away. The delivery fee cost more than the dairy.
options: Delete the delivery apps and walk to the local shop.
Only order when there is a 'Free Delivery' promo code.
Order a side of fries so the delivery fee 'feels' smaller.

C006.text: THE 'PIZZA PARTY' RAISE: Your boss offered a slice of pepperoni instead of a cost-of-living increase.
options: Update your CV and start looking for a new role.
Take three extra slices home in a napkin for dinner.
Thank your boss and ask if there's any garlic dip left.

C007.text: SIDE HUSTLE DELUSION: You spent £200 on knitting supplies to sell scarves. You have made zero scarves.
options: Sell the unused yarn and needles on eBay to recoup costs.
Knit one simple scarf for yourself so it's not a total loss.
Spend another £100 on 'Premium Alpaca Wool' for inspiration.

C008.text: THE OFFICE MICROWAVE: You accidentally blew up a salmon fillet. The social cost is higher than the cleaning fee.
options: Deep clean the microwave and apologize to the floor.
Leave an anonymous 'Out of Order' sign on the door.
Microwave leftover cabbage the following day.

C009.text: LINKEDIN LUNACY: You spent 4 hours writing a 'hustle' post about waking up at 4 AM. You actually woke up at noon.
options: Delete the post and actually do some productive work.
Edit the post to be a 'parody' of hustle culture.
Schedule a follow-up post about the 'Importance of Rest'.

C010.text: THE 'MEETING' THAT COULD HAVE BEEN AN EMAIL: You spent £10 on a 'working lunch' that achieved nothing.
options: Politely decline the next lunch and ask for an agenda.
Make sure you expense the receipt to the company.
Suggest a 3-course dinner to 'really get into the details'.

C011.text: CRYPTO BRO ADVICE: A man with a laser-eye profile picture told you to buy 'Dog-Elon-Mars-Coin'.
options: Block the account and stick to a diversified index fund.
Put £10 in just to see what happens to the chart.
Sell your emergency fund to 'Buy the Dip'.

C012.text: THE SMARTPHONE SMASH: You dropped your phone. The screen looks like a spiderweb. A repair is £200.
options: Pay for the professional repair to keep the phone for 2 more years.
Apply a cheap screen protector to stop the glass from cutting you.
Trade it in for £5 and get the latest model on a 3-year contract.

C013.text: NFT REGRET: You own a digital picture of a sad monkey. It is currently worth £0.04.
options: Accept the loss and use the image as a desktop wallpaper.
List it for £0.05 and hope for a 'market recovery'.
Buy five more 'Sad Monkeys' to lower your average entry price.

C014.text: BATTERY ANXIETY: Your laptop only works when plugged in. You are a 'Wall-Hugger'.
options: Order a replacement battery and install it yourself.
Only use the laptop at your desk at home.
Buy a 50,000mAh power bank that weighs 2kg.

C015.text: THE CLOUD TRAP: You pay for 2TB of storage. 90% of it is blurry photos of your dinner from 2016.
options: Spend an hour deleting duplicates and downgrade your plan.
Download the archives to an old physical hard drive.
Upgrade to the 5TB plan because you're at 99% capacity.

C016.text: DESTINATION WEDDING: Your friend is getting married in a volcano. Flights are £1,200.
options: Send a nice card and a thoughtful gift instead of going.
Go, but stay in a hostel nearby to save on hotel costs.
Put the flights and a designer tuxedo on your credit card.

C017.text: THE BILL SPLIT: You had a salad; they had 4 lobsters. They want to 'split it even'.
options: Firmly say you'll only pay for your own items and tip.
Split the bill but 'forget' to contribute to the tip.
Pay the full split and vent about it on Reddit later.

C018.text: BIRTHDAY PRESSURE: It's a 'Secret Santa' with a £50 limit. You are broke.
options: Suggest a more reasonable £10 limit to the group.
Re-gift a high-quality item you never opened.
Take out a small payday loan to 'look successful'.

C019.text: THE ROUND: It's your turn at the bar. There are 12 people. Your card is sweating.
options: Quietly head home before it's officially your turn.
Suggest everyone buys their own drinks from now on.
Order 12 double whiskeys and hope the card doesn't decline.

C020.text: DATING ON A BUDGET: You suggested 'A walk in the rain' as a first date. They think you're cheap.
options: Suggest a free museum or a gallery visit instead.
Buy two coffees and share your umbrella.
Book a Michelin-star table to prove you're 'not a loser'.

C021.text: THE SCRATCH CARD FANTASY: You won £2. You are now 'statistically likely' to win the jackpot.
options: Take the £2 cash and walk out of the shop.
Exchange the £2 for one more 'lucky' card.
Spend your grocery money on a full roll of scratchies.

C022.text: LIFESTYLE CREEP: You got a £50 bonus and immediately bought a cashmere scarf for £120.
options: Return the scarf and put the £120 in your savings.
Keep the scarf but skip your next three 'treat' coffees.
Buy the matching cashmere gloves to 'complete the look'.

C023.text: THE 'GIRL MATH' ERROR: You bought something for £100 that was £200. You think you 'made' £100.
options: Check your bank balance and acknowledge you spent £100.
Return the item for a full refund of your £100.
Buy a second one because you're 'saving' another £100.

C024.text: CLUTTER CAPITALISM: Your house is full of 'As Seen On TV' vegetable choppers.
options: Donate the unused gadgets to a local charity shop.
Use one to actually chop a vegetable this week.
Order the 'Self-Cleaning' version of the same chopper.

C025.text: THE PENSION BLACK HOLE: You think your retirement plan is 'finding a bag of money'.
options: Set up a small monthly direct debit into a real pension.
Start a 'Change Jar' for your retirement fund.
Buy more lottery tickets to increase your 'finding money' odds.

C026.text: AIR FRYER FEVER: You haven't used your oven in 6 months. You are now a cult member.
options: Cook a vegetable in it occasionally to stay healthy.
Join an online forum to discuss 'Air Fryer Secrets'.
Attempt to air-fry a whole roast turkey on Christmas Day.

C027.text: THE IKEA Labyrinth: You went for a lightbulb; you left with a sofa and 500 tea lights.
options: Stick to the 'Express Exit' and only buy the lightbulb.
Return the sofa but keep the tea lights.
Buy the matching coffee table from the catalog on the way out.

C028.text: THE HEATING COLD WAR: It’s 12°C. You are wearing three jumpers to save 40p.
options: Turn the heating on to 18°C for one hour to dry the air.
Use a hot water bottle to stay warm in one room.
Set fire to your 'Side Hustle' wool supplies for warmth.

C029.text: GROCERY ROULETTE: You bought 'Reduced to Clear' sushi. It expires in 4 minutes.
options: Eat it immediately while standing in the kitchen.
Smell it very carefully before taking a tiny bite.
Save it for your work lunch tomorrow morning.

C030.text: THE LAUNDRY CRISIS: You have no clean socks. You are considering buying new ones instead of washing.
options: Put a load of laundry on and wait for them to dry.
Turn your current socks inside out for one more day.
Buy a 12-pack of 'Designer' socks on your lunch break.

C031.text: THE 'DOORSTEP' SCAM: A man offered to fix your roof for £50. He is currently on your roof with a hammer.
options: Tell him to stop and call a licensed professional.
Ask him to show you a photo of the 'damage' first.
Give him another £50 to 'finish the job quickly'.

C032.text: VINTAGE CLOTHING: You spent £60 on a 'vintage' shirt that smells like a wet basement.
options: Wash it with white vinegar and baking soda.
Wear it as a 'statement piece' and hope nobody smells you.
List it on Depop as 'Distressed Aesthetic' for £80.

C033.text: THE PARKING FINE: You 'just popped in' for 2 minutes. The yellow line didn't care.
options: Pay the fine within 14 days to get the 50% discount.
Try to appeal it with a blurry photo of a broken meter.
Throw the letter away and wait for the bailiffs to call.

C034.text: TREAT CULTURE: You 'treated yourself' every day this week. You are now broke but happy-ish.
options: Declare next week a 'No Spend' week to recover.
Only buy one small treat on Friday as a reward.
Treat yourself to a takeaway because you're 'stressed' about money.

C035.text: THE 'Gamer' SETUP: You spent £3,000 on a PC to play games from 1998.
options: Use the PC for a productive side project or learning code.
Limit your gaming time to two hours an evening.
Buy a £200 'Gaming Chair' to improve your 1998 performance.

C036.text: THE DIY DISASTER: You tried to fix a leak. You have now flooded the kitchen.
options: Turn off the main water valve and call an emergency plumber.
Try to soak up the water with every towel you own.
Watch a 20-minute YouTube tutorial while the water rises.

C037.text: STREAMING OVERLOAD: You pay for Disney+, Netflix, Hulu, and 'Niche-Fish-Videos'.
options: Cancel all but one and rotate them every month.
Share accounts with family members to split costs.
Upgrade to 'Ultra HD Premium' on all four accounts.

C038.text: THE 'FREE' TRIAL: You forgot to cancel. You now own a year of 'Extreme Pogo-Sticking'.
options: Email support immediately and ask for a refund.
Watch at least one video so the money isn't 'wasted'.
Sign up for the 'Extreme Unicycling' trial to compare.

C039.text: THE OFFICE 'BAKE OFF': You bought a supermarket cake and put it on a plate.
options: Own up to it and say you didn't have time to bake.
Dust it with icing sugar to make it look 'homemade'.
Claim it's an 'Artisanal Deconstructed Sponge' from a bakery.

C040.text: THE LOAN TO DAVE: Dave still hasn't paid you the £20 from 2019. He just bought a boat.
options: Politely remind Dave about the money and your bank details.
Ask Dave to buy the next three rounds of drinks.
Ask Dave if you can live on the boat for free.

C041.text: THE PRINTER SCAM: Ink costs more than human blood. Your printer is 'updating'.
options: Use the printer at your local library for 10p a sheet.
Buy compatible third-party ink cartridges online.
Buy a whole new printer because it comes with 'free' ink.

C042.text: THE WELLNESS RETREAT: You spent £1,000 to scream in a forest for a weekend.
options: Go for a free hike in the woods next time you're stressed.
Try 'Forest Bathing' in your local park for an hour.
Book the 'Luxury Silent Retreat' for £2,500 next month.

C043.text: THE 'INVESTMENT' WATCH: It's a gold-plated Casio. It is not an investment.
options: Wear it and enjoy it as a functional timepiece.
Keep it in the original box to prevent scratches.
Try to trade it for a real Rolex on a crypto forum.

C044.text: THE NEW HOBBY: You bought a unicycle. You have fallen off it 40 times.
options: Sell it on Facebook Marketplace while it's still in one piece.
Practice on grass with a helmet and knee pads.
Buy a 'Professional' unicycle to see if that's the problem.

C045.text: THE 'SMART' HOME: Your lights only turn on if you clap three times and shout 'BEANS'.
options: Uninstall the 'smart' bulbs and go back to manual switches.
Program a shortcut on your phone so you don't have to shout.
Add a voice-activated 'Smart Toaster' to the network.

C046.text: THE 'VINTAGE' CAR: It's a 2005 Corolla with a missing door handle.
options: Take it to a local garage for a full safety check.
Glue the handle back on and hope for the best.
List it as a 'Classic Collector's Item' for £5,000.

C047.text: THE BRUNCH BETRAYAL: You were too hungover to go, but they charged you anyway.
options: Check the terms and conditions and ask for a credit note.
Offer to pay half the fee since you didn't eat.
Go anyway and fall asleep in your eggs benedict.

C048.text: THE LUXURY CANDLE: It smells like 'Wealth' but costs £60 and burns for 2 hours.
options: Buy a 10-pack of basic candles from the supermarket.
Only light the expensive candle when you have a date over.
Keep it burning all night to 'manifest' more money.

C049.text: THE REFILL SHOP: You spent £40 on 'Ethical Flour'. It’s just flour.
options: Buy the supermarket 'Value' flour and reuse your old glass jar.
Use the flour to bake a cake for a charity event.
Buy the 'Artisanal Himalayan Salt' for £15 a tin.

C050.text: THE FINAL AUDIT: You are down to your last £10. Payday is in 6 days.
options: Buy a large bag of rice and some frozen peas.
Ask a friend if you can come over for dinner tonight.
Spend the £10 on a 'Mega-Millions' lottery ticket.
